examples.title: 4-to-2 Encoder Logic
examples.description: Pseudocode implementation of a basic 4-to-2 encoder
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Encoder_4to2
INPUTS: I3, I2, I1, I0 (binary inputs)
OUTPUTS: Y1, Y0 (binary outputs)
BEGIN
  // Output Y1 is HIGH when input I3 or I2 is active
  Y1 = I3 OR I2
  
  // Output Y0 is HIGH when input I3 or I1 is active
  Y0 = I3 OR I1
  
  RETURN Y1, Y0
END
examples.explanation: The encoder produces 2-bit binary code corresponding to the position of the active input. Y1 represents the MSB and Y0 represents the LSB of the output.

examples.title: Python Implementation of 4-to-2 Encoder
examples.description: Python function to simulate a 4-to-2 encoder
examples.code:
def encoder_4to2(input_lines):
    '''
    input_lines: list of 4 bits [I3, I2, I1, I0]
    Returns: 2-bit output [Y1, Y0]
    '''
    I3, I2, I1, I0 = input_lines
    
    # Y1 = I3 + I2 (OR operation)
    Y1 = I3 or I2
    
    # Y0 = I3 + I1 (OR operation)
    Y0 = I3 or I1
    
    return [int(Y1), int(Y0)]

# Test cases
test_inputs = [
    [0, 0, 0, 1],  # I0 active -> output 00
    [0, 0, 1, 0],  # I1 active -> output 01
    [0, 1, 0, 0],  # I2 active -> output 10
    [1, 0, 0, 0],  # I3 active -> output 11
]

for inp in test_inputs:
    output = encoder_4to2(inp)
    print(f"Input: {inp} -> Output: {output}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the logical operations of a 4-to-2 encoder. Each test case shows the output binary code for different active input positions.

examples.title: C Implementation of Priority Encoder
examples.description: C program implementing an 8-to-3 priority encoder with valid output
examples.explanation: This C implementation shows how priority encoders work by checking inputs from highest index downward and breaking at the first active input found, ensuring proper handling of multiple simultaneous inputs.

examples.title: Java Implementation of 4-to-2 Encoder
examples.description: Java class implementing a 4-to-2 encoder with enable input
examples.explanation: This Java class demonstrates encoder implementation with enable input control and input validation. The encode method returns the 2-bit binary output corresponding to the active input line.
